message:
fix(ci): use formula names instead of paths for brew audit

The `brew audit [path ...]` syntax is deprecated. The Homebrew audit
command now requires formula names instead of file paths.

This fix:
- Taps the local checkout first with `brew tap dicklesworthstone/tap .`
- Uses the full formula name format `dicklesworthstone/tap/$name` for audit

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -44,6 +44,9 @@ jobs:
 
       - name: Audit all formulas
         run: |
+          # Tap the local checkout so we can audit by formula name
+          brew tap dicklesworthstone/tap .
+
           shopt -s nullglob
           formulas=(Formula/*.rb)
 
@@ -60,7 +63,7 @@ jobs:
             name=$(basename "$formula" .rb)
             echo "::group::Auditing $name"
 
-            if brew audit --strict --online "$formula" 2>&1; then
+            if brew audit --strict --online "dicklesworthstone/tap/$name" 2>&1; then
               echo "Audit passed for $name"
             else
               echo "::error::Audit failed for $name"
